steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: Arizona pocket mice avoid foraging in bright moonlight (costs exceed benefits), while Azara's night monkeys forage despite it. The passage explains this reversal: their “heavy reliance on visual foraging results in a different balance of reward and risk.”

steps.title: Apply to the choices
steps.content: The different balance means night monkeys gain substantial foraging rewards from increased moonlight visibility, so their costs relative to benefits are lower than for pocket mice—exactly what C describes. The other three choices either reverse the predation detection pattern, introduce unsupported claims about comparative risk levels, or claim reduced foraging success for night monkeys. That matches choice C — C is correct.
